nameMeanings.name: Known
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Down): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Eoghain (in Connacht) ‘son of Eoghan’, a personal name meaning ‘born of the yew’, and Mac Eoin (in east Ulster), meaning ‘son of John’. See McEwan . This name has also been associated with Ó Ceotháin in Fermanagh.

nameMeanings.name: Not
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Nathan   and   Nathaniel .

en.lifeSummary: Not Known was born in 1179, in Blacon, Chester, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. She married Hugh de Raby in 1194, in Chester, Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter.
